Recipe.name: Steamed BBQ Pork Buns (Char Siu Bao)
Recipe.Notes: Baking powder goes in after the 2-hour rise, not before. Cornstarch is what gives the buns that pillowy dim sum texture. Steam in batches — don't let water touch the buns.

Ingredient.name: shallots or red onion, finely chopped

Steps.Step: Dissolve yeast in warm water. Mix sifted flour and cornstarch with yeast mixture, sugar, and oil using a dough hook. Knead until smooth. Cover and rest 2 hours.

Make filling: heat oil in wok over medium-high. Stir-fry onion briefly, reduce heat, add sugar, soy sauces, sesame oil. Cook until bubbling. Add stock and flour, stir until thickened. Fold in roast pork. Cool completely.
Add baking powder to risen dough and knead gently until smooth. Add 1–2 tsp water if needed. Rest 15 minutes. Cut ten 4×4-inch parchment squares. Bring steamer to boil.
Roll dough into a log and divide into 10 pieces. Press each into a 4½-inch disc — thicker center, thinner edges. Add filling and pleat until sealed on top.
Place each bun on a parchment square. Steam in two batches, 12 minutes each over high heat.
